backlog.title: Build core platform database schema
backlog.description: Create SQLite database schema for: clients, points_balances (per program per client), award_searches, booking_pipeline, transfer_bonuses, and flight_inventory tables. Use SQLAlchemy ORM models in Python. Include seed data with 3 sample clients.
acceptance_criteria: All tables created with proper foreign keys
Seed data script with 3 sample clients and their points balances

backlog.title: Build FastAPI backend server with all core routes
backlog.description: Create a FastAPI server (platform/src/api/server.py) with routes for: GET /clients, POST /clients, GET /clients/{id}/points, POST /points/update, GET /awards/search, GET /pipeline, POST /pipeline/add, GET /transfer-bonuses. All routes return proper JSON. Include CORS for local frontend.
acceptance_criteria: All 8 routes functional and returning mock data
Pydantic schemas for all request/response models

backlog.title: Build award availability scraper for United MileagePlus
backlog.description: Build a Python scraper (platform/src/scrapers/united.py) that queries United MileagePlus award calendar availability. Use requests + BeautifulSoup. Target route: JFK to any Saver award in J or F class. Output structured JSON: {route, date, cabin, miles_required, taxes, available_seats, scraped_at}. Include rate limiting and retry logic.
acceptance_criteria: Scraper runs without crashing on 5 test queries
Returns structured award data as JSON
Rate limiting: max 1 request per 3 seconds

backlog.title: Build complete platform dashboard UI
backlog.description: Build the main District Award Travel business dashboard as a single-page HTML/CSS/JavaScript app (platform/public/index.html). Must include: 1) Client list with points balances per program 2) Booking pipeline with kanban-style stages (Searching, Seat Found, Holding, Booked, Complete) 3) Transfer bonus tracker with countdown timers 4) Award search results feed 5) Dark theme matching brand (#080c10 bg, #00ff88 accent). Fetch live data from FastAPI backend at localhost:8000.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches live data from API (with graceful fallback to mock data)

backlog.title: Build client points tracker with transfer math
backlog.description: Build platform/public/clients.html — a detailed client management page. Each client shows: all loyalty program balances, current transfer bonus opportunities with math (e.g., 'Transfer 50k Amex now: get 65k ANA = $1,105 value at 1.7cpp'), booking history, and recommended next action. Include a form to add/edit client points balances.
acceptance_criteria: Transfer bonus math computed and displayed
Value calculations shown in cpp (cents per point)

backlog.title: Build Playwright login automation for ANA Mileage Club
backlog.description: Build platform/src/automation/ana_login.js using Playwright. Must: 1) Launch headless Chromium 2) Navigate to ana.co.jp award search 3) Handle login with credentials from .env 4) Search for award availability on a given route/date 5) Extract available seats as structured JSON 6) Handle 2FA if present (log warning, save screenshot) 7) Use playwright-extra with stealth plugin to avoid bot detection.
acceptance_criteria: Extracts award seat data as JSON
Screenshot saved on any error for debugging

backlog.title: Build transfer bonus monitor for all major issuers
backlog.description: Build platform/src/automation/transfer_monitor.js — a Node.js script that checks all known credit card transfer bonus promotions. Maintain a JSON database (tasks/transfer_bonuses.json) of current bonuses with: issuer, to_program, bonus_percent, expires_date, last_verified. Scrape or check known bonus pages for Amex, Chase, Capital One, Citi. Alert if new bonus detected by writing to tasks/alerts.json.
acceptance_criteria: Detects NEW bonuses vs previously known
Writes to alerts.json for new discoveries
Runs as a standalone cron-able script

backlog.title: Build nightly orchestrator report email
backlog.description: Build platform/src/reports/morning_report.py — a script that compiles everything the agents found overnight and formats it as an HTML email summary. Include: new award seats found, transfer bonuses detected, clients with bookable opportunities, pipeline status changes, and any errors. Send via SMTP (use environment variables for credentials). Run as final step of nightly orchestration.
acceptance_criteria: Pulls data from all results JSON files
Sends via SMTP with env-var credentials
Subject line: 'DAT Morning Brief — {date} — {N} opportunities found'
Falls back to saving HTML to logs/ if SMTP not configured
